kind: service-layer + bounded live verification (NOT a packaged-installer smoke)
disclaimer: Dev/service-layer live proof of the real LLM path (real opencode.exe + real provider behind it). This is legitimate evidence for the LLM path but is NOT the packaged-installer smoke test; no packaged-build PASS is claimed here.

legs.title: Template re-run (RE4) + session resume (S4) — service layer, no live provider
proven: RE4: template save→re-run through composed deps.extensions resolves ${input.*} deterministically (repeatable), and a missing required input is a CLEAN invalid_input error (not a throw/quarantine).
S4: after a simulated restart the view was rebuilt from store replay through the real mapper+reducer (had-in-memory-before=false, rebuilt toolCalls=1 fileMutations=1).

legs.title: Live critical path (EV token stream + honest completed terminal + file mutation)
proven: startLiveCoworkService spawned the REAL pinned opencode.exe and reached pinned-and-healthy readiness.
Supervisor honest liveness after start: isAlive=true baseUrl!=null=true.
1a simple prompt: 1 token EV(s) over the boundary; honest terminal=completed (EV7).
1b file-write prompt: tool_call (EV3) + file_mutation (EV4) over the boundary AND ready.txt actually written to disk.
S4 resume: view rebuilt from live store replay (terminal=none) via the real mapper+reducer.
stop(): child torn down — baseUrl=null=true, isAlive=false (no orphan).
commands: node --import tsx tools/verify/run-wave-c.mjs (leg 1 live critical path)
notes: S4 rebuilt view terminal did not match completed (replay shape variance).

legs.title: Provider-error leg (PR7 — invalid credential surfaces a distinct, secret-free error)
proven: Error-map (no live): 401→auth_invalid, 429→rate_limited, 408→timeout, 503→unavailable — static secret-free messages + recovery.
Live stack started with a bogus key injected via the SAME env/keyring seam (health does not validate the key).
Invalid credential → DISTINCT error EV (recovery=retry) + honest terminal=errored (no fabricated completed). Message is secret-free.
